untranslatable_reason: Signal requires pair-trading spread construction (long stock 1 minus short stock 2) and Ornstein-Uhlenbeck half-life parameter estimation via linear regression. These are multi-asset portfolio mechanics and custom statistical estimates not expressible as single-instrument indicator conditions. Z-score is a proxy for mean reversion detection but does not capture the spread construction, half-life calculation, or pair-specific mean-reverting process validation required by the original rule.
translation_notes: This is a pairs trading strategy requiring: (1) two correlated securities with spread = P1 - P2, (2) estimation of OU process parameter θ via regression, (3) half-life = ln(2)/θ calculation, (4) dynamic threshold based on half-life. The zscore_20 condition provides a generic mean-reversion entry/exit scaffold, but the true signal logic (spread identification, OU process validation, half-life decay expectation) cannot be expressed in single-asset indicator conditions. Backtest engine must support pair trading mechanics and custom parameter estimation to properly implement this strategy.
